data.context:
Task task4:
  Objective: Develop a recruiter email generator for sending interview invitations and follow-up messages.
  Result: ```python
import smtplib
from email.mime.text import MIMEText
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

class EmailGenerator:
    def __init__(self, smtp_server, smtp_port, sender_email, sender_password):
        self.smtp_server = smtp_server
        self.smtp_port = smtp_port
        self.sender_email = sender_email
        self.sender_password = sender_password

    def create_invitation_email(self, recipient_email, candidate_name, role_name, interview_date, interview_time, interviewer_name, interview_details):
        """Generates an interview invitation email."""
        subject = f"Interview Invitation for {role_name} at [Company Name]"
        body = f"""Dear {candidate_name},

We are pleased to invite you to an interview for the {role_name} position at [Company Name].

Date: {interview_date}
Time: {interview_time}
Interviewer: {interviewer_name}
Details: {interview_details}

Please confirm your availability by replying to this email.

We look forward to speaking with you.

Sincerely,
[Your Name]
[Your Title]
[Company Name]
"""
        return self.create_email(recipient_email, subject, body)

    def create_followup_email(self, recipient_email, candidate_name, interviewer_name, role_name):
        """Generates a follow-up email after the interview."""
        subject = f"Following Up on Your Interview for {role_name} at [Company Name]"
        body = f"""Dear {candidate_name},

Thank you for taking the time to interview for the {role_name} position at [Company Name] with {interviewer_name}.

We appreciate you sharing your experience and qualifications with us. We are currently reviewing all candidates and will be in touch soon regarding the next steps.

Sincerely,
[Your Name]
[Your Title]
[Company Name]
"""
        return self.create_email(recipient_email, subject, body)

    def create_rejection_email(self, recipient_email, candidate_name, role_name):
        """Generates a rejection email."""
        subject = f"Update on Your Application for {role_name} at [Company Name]"
        body = f"""Dear {candidate_name},

Thank you for your interest in the {role_name} position at [Company Name]. We appreciate you taking the time to apply.

We have carefully reviewed your application and have decided to move forward with other candidates whose qualifications and experience are a closer match for the requirements of this role.

We wish you the best in your job search.

Sincerely,
[Your Name]
[Your Title]
[Company Name]
"""
        return self.create_email(recipient_email, subject, body)

    def create_offer_email(self, recipient_email, candidate_name, role_name, salary, start_date):
      """Generates an offer email."""
      subject = f"Job Offer: {role_name} at [Company Name]"
      body = f"""Dear {candidate_name},

We are delighted to offer you the position of {role_name} at [Company Name]!

We were very impressed with your qualifications and experience during the interview process.

Here are the details of the offer:

* Role: {role_name}
* Salary: {salary}
* Start Date: {start_date}

A formal offer letter with more details will follow shortly. Please let us know if you have any questions.

We are excited about the possibility of you joining our team!

Sincerely,
[Your Name]
[Your Title]
[Company Name]
"""
      return self.create_email(recipient_email, subject, body)

    def create_email(self, recipient_email, subject, body):
        """Creates and returns a MIMEText email object."""
        msg = MIMEText(body)
        msg['Subject'] = subject
        msg['From'] = self.sender_email
        msg['To'] = recipient_email
        return msg

    def send_email(self, recipient_email, subject, body):
        """Sends an email."""
        msg = self.create_email(recipient_email, subject, body)
        try:
            with smtplib.SMTP(self.smtp_server, self.smtp_port) as server:
                server.starttls()
                server.login(self.sender_email, self.sender_password)
                server.sendmail(self.sender_email, recipient_email, msg.as_string())
            print(f"Email sent to {recipient_email}")
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Error sending email: {e}")


if __name__ == '__main__':
    # Example Usage
    smtp_server = 'smtp.gmail.com'  # Replace with your SMTP server
    smtp_port = 587  # Replace with your SMTP port
    sender_email = 'your_email@gmail.com'  # Replace with your email
    sender_password = 'your_password'  # Replace with your password (or app password)

    email_generator = EmailGenerator(smtp_server, smtp_port, sender_email, sender_password)

    # Example: Sending an interview invitation
    recipient_email = 'candidate@example.com'
    candidate_name = 'Jane Smith'
    role_name = 'Software Engineer'
    interview_date = datetime.now() + timedelta(days=2)
    interview_date_str = interview_date.strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
    interview_time = "10:00 AM"
    interviewer_name = 'John Doe'
    interview_details = "Please join the Zoom meeting: [Zoom Link]"

    email_generator.send_email(recipient_email,
                               f"Interview Invitation for {role_name} at [Company Name]",
                               email_generator.create_invitation_email(recipient_email, candidate_name, role_name, interview_date_str, interview_time, interviewer_name, interview_details).get_payload()) #Fixed: Correctly pass recipient email, subject, and body

    # Example: Sending a follow-up email
    email_generator.send_email(recipient_email,
                               f"Following Up on Your Interview for {role_name} at [Company Name]",
                               email_generator.create_followup_email(recipient_email, candidate_name, interviewer_name, role_name).get_payload())
    
    # Example: Sending a rejection email
    email_generator.send_email(recipient_email,
                               f"Update on Your Application for {role_name} at [Company Name]",
                               email_generator.create_rejection_email(recipient_email, candidate_name, role_name).get_payload())

    # Example: Sending an offer email
    email_generator.send_email(recipient_email,
                               f"Job Offer: {role_name} at [Company Name]",
                               email_generator.create_offer_email(recipient_email, candidate_name, role_name, "$120,000", "2024-08-01").get_payload())
```
data.next_objective:
Task task2:
  Objective: Build a follow-up question generation component that dynamically creates questions based on candidate responses.
data.result:
```python
class InterviewQuestionGenerator:
    def __init__(self, role_requirements):
        """
        Initializes the InterviewQuestionGenerator with the role requirements.

        Args:
            role_requirements (str): A description of the role requirements.
        """
        self.role_requirements = role_requirements

    def generate_technical_questions(self, job_description):
        """
        Generates technical interview questions based on the job description.

        Args:
            job_description (str): The job description for the role.

        Returns:
            list: A list of technical interview questions.
        """
        technical_questions = []

        # Extract technical skills from job description
        skills = self.extract_skills(job_description)

        # Generate questions based on the extracted skills
        for skill in skills:
            technical_questions.append(f"Explain your experience with {skill}.")
            technical_questions.append(f"Describe a project where you used {skill}.")
            technical_questions.append(f"What are the advantages and disadvantages of using {skill}?")
            technical_questions.append(f"How do you troubleshoot issues related to {skill}?")

        # Add some general technical questions
        technical_questions.append("Describe your experience with software development methodologies (e.g., Agile, Waterfall).")
        technical_questions.append("Explain the difference between a stack and a queue.")
        technical_questions.append("What is the purpose of version control systems like Git?")
        technical_questions.append("How do you approach debugging a complex software issue?")
        technical_questions.append("Explain the concept of object-oriented programming.")

        return technical_questions

    def generate_behavioral_questions(self):
        """
        Generates behavioral interview questions based on the role requirements.

        Returns:
            list: A list of behavioral interview questions.
        """
        behavioral_questions = []

        # Generate questions based on common behavioral competencies
        behavioral_questions.append("Tell me about a time you had to work under pressure. How did you handle it?")
        behavioral_questions.append("Describe a situation where you had to deal with a difficult colleague. How did you resolve the conflict?")
        behavioral_questions.append("Give an example of a time you failed. What did you learn from it?")
        behavioral_questions.append("Tell me about a time you had to make a quick decision with limited information.")
        behavioral_questions.append("Describe a situation where you had to lead a team to achieve a common goal.")
        behavioral_questions.append("How do you handle receiving negative feedback?")
        behavioral_questions.append("Tell me about a time you went above and beyond what was expected of you.")
        behavioral_questions.append("Describe your approach to problem-solving.")
        behavioral_questions.append("How do you stay motivated and productive when facing challenging tasks?")
        behavioral_questions.append("Explain how you prioritize tasks when faced with multiple deadlines.")
        behavioral_questions.append("Describe a time when you had to adapt to a significant change in the workplace.")

        return behavioral_questions

    def extract_skills(self, job_description):
        """
        Extracts technical skills from the job description.

        Args:
            job_description (str): The job description.

        Returns:
            list: A list of extracted skills.
        """
        # This is a placeholder.  A more sophisticated implementation would use NLP techniques.
        # For now, let's just return some hardcoded skills based on common keywords.
        skills = []
        job_description = job_description.lower()
        if "python" in job_description:
            skills.append("Python")
        if "java" in job_description:
            skills.append("Java")
        if "c++" in job_description:
            skills.append("C++")
        if "javascript" in job_description:
            skills.append("JavaScript")
        if "sql" in job_description:
            skills.append("SQL")
        if "aws" in job_description:
            skills.append("AWS")
        if "azure" in job_description:
            skills.append("Azure")
        if "docker" in job_description:
            skills.append("Docker")
        if "kubernetes" in job_description:
            skills.append("Kubernetes")
        if "machine learning" in job_description:
            skills.append("Machine Learning")
        if "data science" in job_description:
            skills.append("Data Science")
        return skills

if __name__ == '__main__':
    # Example Usage
    role_requirements = "Software Engineer with experience in Python and AWS."
    job_description = """
    We are looking for a Software Engineer to join our team.
    The ideal candidate should have experience with Python, AWS, and SQL.
    Experience with Docker and Kubernetes is a plus.
    """

    question_generator = InterviewQuestionGenerator(role_requirements)

    technical_questions = question_generator.generate_technical_questions(job_description)
    print("Technical Questions:")
    for question in technical_questions:
        print(f"- {question}")

    behavioral_questions = question_generator.generate_behavioral_questions()
    print("\nBehavioral Questions:")
    for question in behavioral_questions:
        print(f"- {question}")
```
